channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At UN climate talks, a draft of the deal gives little clarity on climate cash for developing nations
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — A new draft text released early Thursday which will form the basis of any deal reached at United Nations climate talks on money for developing countries to transition to clean energy and adapt to climate change left out a crucial sticking point: how much wealthy nations will
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why people are protesting over a deadly roof collapse in Serbia
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — The deadly collapse of a concrete roof at a rail station in Serbia has raised tensions across the Balkan county, fueling widespread anger toward the government and protests after the failure claimed 15 lives in the northern city of Novi Sad.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chris Stapleton wins 4 CMA Awards, but Morgan Wallen gets entertainer of the year
item.description: Chris Stapleton took home four Country Music Association Awards and hardly left the stage on Wednesday night, while an absent Morgan Wallen was shut out all evening until taking the night's biggest prize, entertainer of the year.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Judge keeps death penalty a possibility for man charged in killings of 4 Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The death penalty will remain a possibility for a man charged with murder in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s hunt for Treasury secretary down to the wire
item.description: The jockeying for President-elect Trump’s pick to run the all-important Treasury Department is coming down to the wire. Trump’s economic team will be tasked with imposing an aggressive tariff regime meant to shake up global trade while keeping financial markets calm amid the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions has testified that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks
item.description: “A drastic reduction in federal regulations provides sound industrial logic for mass head-count reductions across the federal bureaucracy,” the pair wrote in the op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla. (AP) — A new study has found that systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in Native American turnout, particularly for presidential elections.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lyft driver allegedly tried to scam woman after 'imaginary child' peed on seat
item.description: "My gosh, I walked through the grass. Was there something on my shoes?" she said. "So I looked at the bottom of my shoes: completely clean.
item.dc:creator: Seth Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Man wins $650K on lottery ticket using numbers tied to late wife
item.description: A Chicago man won a $650,000 Lucky Day Lotto jackpot prize after playing numbers related to his late wife.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Residents fear ‘attack of the trees’ as aging giants wreak havoc
item.description: “It happens every day,” Bob Jackson said. “The tree limbs are falling. It doesn’t have to be windy; it doesn’t have to be stormy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Mountain lion killed by Missouri deer hunter, investigation underway
item.description: "The Department does not condone the indiscriminate killing of mountain lions, but we understand the need to allow people to protect livestock and human safety if they are threatened," said MDC.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Unhappy with the 2024 election results? This Italian village is offering $1 homes to Americans
item.description: Americans who are unhappy with the 2024 election results and have fantasized about leaving the country could turn that dream into a reality thanks to a recent offer from an Italian village.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DEA targets 'substantial drug trafficking operation' in central Indiana
item.description: The early morning drug busts resulted in more than a dozen arrests. Warrants were served in at least 15 locations.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Texas officials vote to back optional Bible lessons for K-5 classes: Reports
item.description: Critics have said the material disproportionately focuses on Christianity, only briefly mentioning other religions such as Judaism or Islam.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man convicted of killing Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: The Venezuelan man convicted in the killing of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to nominate professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is nominating the billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Today's entry-level employees not ready for the job, survey of executives says
item.description: (KRON) -- Entry-level Gen Z workers aren't prepared for the demands of the modern workplace, according to a majority of executives and workers surveyed in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: John Stamos wears bald cap to show support for Dave Coulier amid cancer diagnosis
item.description: John Stamos is showing his support for his former "Full House" co-star, Dave Coulier, amid his cancer diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Rare coin found in piece of furniture sells for $2.52M at auction
item.description: A rare 17th Century coin sold at auction for a record-breaking $2.52 million eight years after it was found in an old cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Trial begins for Florida man in human smuggling case after freezing deaths of Indian family at Canada-US border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — Federal prosecutors plan to argue at a Minnesota trial that two men were part of a criminal network stretching from India to Canada that smuggled families seeking better lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmaker says State Supt. Ryan Walters' 'Prayer to the Nation' video is indoctrination
item.description: A recorded prayer was emailed in an unlisted YouTube link to Oklahoma school superintendents yesterday, telling them to have the video shared in classrooms and with parents.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WGN 9
